justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Scheduling lag metric gap identified as primary SLO gap with explicit 'renewal_scheduling_lag_seconds histogram' recommendation, (2) Retry tracking gap clearly identified with retry_attempts_total counter and retry_lag_seconds histogram, (3) Current alert explicitly critiqued as lagging indicator/symptom-based ('errors > 10 in 5min is a poor proxy'), (4) Specific metric recommendations provided (scheduling lag histogram, SLO violations counter, queue depth gauge, retry attempts counter, retry lag histogram, final failures counter), (5) SLO burn rate alerting recommended with rolling windows (1h, 6h, 24h), (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough identification of the scheduling lag gap as THE metric for the 99.9%/1-hour SLO, called out explicitly as unmeasurable
Retry lifecycle gap comprehensively covered — not just the counter but also retry_lag_seconds for the 24-hour SLO
Clear critique of the existing alert as a lagging indicator that fires after damage is done, correctly identifying it as symptom-based rather than SLO-based
Burn rate alerting recommendation is specific and actionable with rolling window periods
Goes beyond minimum requirements with queue depth, scheduler heartbeat, dead letter queue, and reconciliation recommendations
Strong operational framing — consistently ties gaps back to on-call impact and revenue loss rather than abstract best practices
